pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he's in the kitchen or i'll hear his keys in the door, but then it just hits me all over again. the house feels like a tomb. i don't even know why i'm botherng with getting up or eating anymore. what's the point of just... existing in this silence?

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. everything we do, every struggle, every feeling... it's just noise before the heat death of the universe. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep going when the math just proves it's all zero in the end?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back today and it's just... over. three years of work down the drain. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research and now that it's gone, i just feel empty. i'm so tired of fighting for a future that feels like it's constantly slipping away. i just want everything to stop. i just want to sleep and not have to wake up to this pressure anymore.

pool.baseline: The candle burns low, a flickering ghost in this hollow room... and I find I can no longer bear the weight of the silence. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I am but a shadow wandering through a life that no longer holds meaning. Everything feels so heavy, so terribly, endlessly gray. I feel as though I am drifting toward the edge of a great, dark sea, and the thought of simply... letting go... of this crushing existence... it feels like the only peace left to me.
